_comment_meeting_name: REQUIRED. The text to look for on your calendar (case-insensitive) and the human-readable name shown in the prep doc. Examples: 'Sprint Review', 'Director 1:1', 'Team Standup', 'How I AI'.
_comment_user_name: Optional but recommended. The name that appears after '## ' in your team's standup files for your section (e.g. 'Don', 'Caroline'). If empty, the standup-bullet scan is skipped.
_comment_meeting_cadence_days: Optional. How many days between meetings, used as the fallback window when state.json hasn't recorded a previous meeting yet. Defaults to 14.
_comment_lookahead_days: Optional. How many days ahead to look on the calendar when wired up to a morning briefing. Defaults to 3 (i.e. trigger up to 3 days before the meeting).
_comment_filter_keywords: Optional. If non-empty, the standup-bullet and scratch-folder scans only include items matching at least one keyword (case-insensitive substring). Leave empty to capture all activity (the default for sprint reviews and status meetings). For a narrow-topic meeting (AI-only round, security review, etc.), supply matching keywords here.
_comment_skills_dir: Optional. Where Cursor stores your skills. Defaults to '%USERPROFILE%\.cursor\skills'.
_comment_scratch_dir: Optional. Your working / scratch folder where AI-driven or exploratory work lives. Defaults to 'C:\GitRepos\.scratch'. Set to null/remove if you don't have one.
_comment_standup_dir: Optional. Where your team's standup notes live. Set this if your team keeps a shared standup file with a '## <name>' section per person. No sensible default — leave unset to skip the standup scan.
_comment_git_repos: Optional. Array of repo paths to scan for your commits. Empty = skip git scan. Example: ['C:\\GitRepos\\repo-a', 'C:\\GitRepos\\repo-b'].
_comment_git_author: Optional. The git author name to filter on. Defaults to user_name. Set explicitly if your standup name and your `git config user.name` differ.
_comment_ticket_pattern: Optional. Regex used to extract ticket IDs from commit messages and folder names. Defaults to a 6-to-8-digit number. Override for Jira-style or other formats (e.g. '[A-Z]+-\\d+' for 'PROJ-1234').
_comment_master_list_path: Optional. Path to a personal markdown file where you keep a running list of projects (one ## heading per project). When set, the skill cross-references discoveries against this list and offers to add new entries or update statuses. Leave empty to skip.
_comment_prep_dir: Optional. Root folder where prep docs are written. The skill creates a subfolder named after the meeting slug. Defaults to '%USERPROFILE%\Downloads\Meeting-Prep'.
_comment_state_dir: Optional. Where state files are stored. Defaults to '%USERPROFILE%\.config\since-last-meeting'. Each meeting gets its own '<slug>.json' inside this folder.
_comment_agent_transcripts_dir: Optional. Where Cursor stores agent transcripts for the workspace you most often work in. Cursor folder names encode the workspace path. To find yours, browse '%USERPROFILE%\.cursor\projects\' and pick the right subfolder. Leave empty to skip the transcript scan.
_comment_env_file: Optional. Path to a .env file with credentials used by an upstream morning-briefing skill (Microsoft Graph token, etc.). This skill itself doesn't read the calendar — that's the briefing's job. Defaults to '%USERPROFILE%\.env'.
_comment_meetings: Optional ADVANCED mode. If you want one install to drive multiple meetings, replace the top-level config with a 'meetings' array. Each entry can override any key above; top-level keys serve as defaults. Example below — uncomment if needed.
